content.problemStatement:
Chef is taking a cooking exam. The exam consists of NNN multiple-choice questions numbered 111 through NNN. In each question, Chef may choose one out of four choices: A, B, C and D, or leave the question unanswered.

Answering a question correctly gives Chef 111 point. Not answering a question or answering it incorrectly gives him 000 points. However, there is a penalty for wrong answers. Specifically, the questions are graded one by one from question 111 to question NNN; for each iii (1≤i≤N−11 \le i \le N-11≤i≤N−1), if Chef answered the iii-th question incorrectly, his answer to the next question (the (i+1)(i+1)(i+1)-th question) is discarded without being graded. If an answer is discarded, Chef gets 000 points for it automatically, but even if it was wrong, the next answer is not discarded. There is no penalty for answering the last question incorrectly or for not answering some questions.

You are given the correct answers to all NNN questions and Chef's answers. Calculate the total number of points Chef scored on the exam.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a single string SSS with length NNN. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of this string denotes the correct answer to the iii-th question.
The third line contains a single string UUU with length NNN. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of this string is 'N' if Chef did not answer the iii-th question; otherwise, it denotes Chef's answer to this question.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — Chef's score.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
6
ABCDAB
ABCDAB
8
DDCABCCA
DNCBBBBA
3
CDD
NDC
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef's answers were all correct, so he got a full score.
Example case 2: Chef did not answer question 222 and he answered questions 444 and 666 incorrectly, which resulted in discarding answers to questions 555 and 777. Note that even though Chef's answer to question 777 was wrong, it was discarded, so it did not result in discarding his answer to question 888.
Example case 3: Chef did not answer the first question and he answered the last question incorrectly, but there is no penalty for that.

hidden.input:
3
6
ABCDAB
ABCDAB
8
DDCABCCA
DNCBBBBA
3
CDD
NDC
